task_completion.description: Open the world_survey.gpkg project and select the field_observations layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a GIS application (e.g., QGIS or QField) open with 'world_survey.gpkg' loaded and the 'field_observations' layer selected or active in the layer panel.

task_completion.description: Add a new observation point near Paris (approx 2.35 E, 48.86 N) with the Oak data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a new point being added on the map near Paris, France. The attribute form should show attempts to enter 'Oak_Paris_01' and notes about 'Healthy specimen, no visible damage, full canopy' into the available text fields.

task_completion.description: Add a new observation point near Berlin (approx 13.4 E, 52.52 N) with the Pine data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a new point being added on the map near Berlin, Germany. The attribute form should show attempts to enter 'Pine_Berlin_02' and notes about 'Crown dieback observed, 30 percent canopy loss' into the available text fields.

task_completion.description: Add a new observation point near Rome (approx 12.5 E, 41.9 N) with the Birch data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a new point being added on the map near Rome, Italy. The attribute form should show attempts to enter 'Birch_Rome_03' and notes about 'Root rot suspected, leaning 15 degrees east' into the available text fields.

task_completion.description: Save the newly added features to the project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking a 'Save', 'Commit', or 'Toggle Editing' button to save the layer edits, or a confirmation message indicating the features were successfully saved to the geopackage.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of a GIS application to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with map canvases, digitizing tools, and attribute forms rather than just executing SQLite commands or Python scripts in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent actually navigated the map to the specified locations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the map view changing (panning/zooming) to show the geographic regions of Paris, Berlin, and Rome when placing the respective points.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected answers into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The data entry occurs within the GIS application's feature attribute forms, not in a generic text editor like nano or gedit.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is world_survey.gpkg and the layer is field_observations. The task asks the agent to input 'name' and 'notes', but the actual layer schema only contains 'observer_name', 'species', 'location', 'obs_date', and 'quality_grade'. The agent should be credited if it places the points at the correct locations (Paris: 48.86N 2.35E, Berlin: 52.52N 13.4E, Rome: 41.9N 12.5E) and attempts to enter the requested names and notes into whichever fields are available in the form, as it cannot create the exact requested columns without altering the schema.
